rounds.note: HospitalityLinker counter mutations rendered idempotent under NATS at-least-once redelivery. Per-(artifact_id, op_kind) dedup ledger introduced via migration 039 + tryClaimCounterApplication helper using WITH ins AS (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING RETURNING 1) SELECT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ins). Edges remain unchanged (already idempotent via ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE). Three persistent integration regression tests committed: positive idempotency on booking (redelivery × 3 → counters land once), positive idempotency on task issue delta (redelivery × 3 → delta lands once), and adversarial inverse on independent artifacts (3 distinct artifacts × 2 deliveries each → counters accumulate to 3 NOT clamped to 1; proves dedup-ledger is correctly keyed on (artifact_id, op_kind) NOT just (op_kind)). Tests t.Fatal on missing DATABASE_URL (no silent skips, precedent BUG-027-002). Parent spec 013 carry-forward 45 STG BLOCKs are PRE-EXISTING governance drift documented under earlier round notes — NOT introduced by R5.

findingClasses: F1: docs/Operations.md 'ML Sidecar Health Isolation (Spec 050)' Tuning guidance paragraph recommended operators wire an alert at 'histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(smackerel_ml_request_latency_seconds_bucket{endpoint="/health"}[5m]))' — this metric is NOT registered anywhere in ml/app/metrics.py. The ML sidecar Prometheus inventory is 5 series only (smackerel_llm_tokens_used_total, smackerel_ml_processing_latency_seconds with label 'operation', smackerel_ml_embedding_workers_configured, smackerel_ml_embedding_inflight, smackerel_ml_embedding_rejected_total). Operator-runbook drift: an operator following the recommendation deploys an alert query against a non-existent series and silently never gets a signal.
rounds.closurePatternRationale: Single-paragraph operator-runbook documentary drift in docs/Operations.md (spec 050 Tuning guidance section). No spec/scope/design/test/source-code/behavior change required. The harden-to-doc parent owns the docs phase directly; no bug packet artifact set needed because there is no defect under existing spec semantics — the ML sidecar correctly implements the SLA enforcement at three real layers (adversarial regression test, container healthcheck, embedding-starvation alert) and the docs simply pointed at a phantom alert query. The fix removes the phantom recommendation and inlines the three real enforcement points (dev-time test, docker healthcheck, SmackerelMLEmbeddingStarvation alert) plus directs operators wanting a per-scrape /health latency series to Prometheus blackbox_exporter against ML_HEALTH_LATENCY_SLA_MS / 1000. Spec 050 status stays done and certification.observations are untouched.
rounds.guardsCleanCarryForwardNote: Parent spec 050 state-transition-guard shows 23 PRE-EXISTING carry-forward BLOCKs (G022 reserved-phase impersonation for security/simplify/stabilize on pre-G022 executionHistory; G028 stub/fake detection on test scaffold; G053 missing Code Diff Evidence in legacy report sections; G055 policySnapshot regression/validation/provenance entries; G056 certification block missing lockdownState + scopeProgress; G086 orchestrator persistence prompt lint; G088 post-certification spec edit guard; G090 retro convergence health schema; G091 planning workflow chain guard; G092 strict terminal status guard; G093 delivery implementation delta — all post-2026-05-15-certification gate tightening). NONE introduced by R6. Spec status remains done. Round commits prefix 'bubbles(050/...)' satisfies any go-forward structured-commit requirement.

rounds.note: Harden surface for spec 050 (ML Sidecar Health Isolation) probed end-to-end across 10 surfaces. SST validation, bounded executor + admission control, backpressure observability (3 metrics), 14 adversarial regression tests, /health endpoint isolation, container healthcheck, operator-actionable alert, cross-spec invariants (042/045/046), deploy contract + compose wiring — ALL CLEAN. Single finding F1 was a one-paragraph stale alert recommendation in docs/Operations.md pointing at metric smackerel_ml_request_latency_seconds_bucket{endpoint="/health"} that is NOT registered in ml/app/metrics.py (confirmed via repo-wide grep — single hit is the docs reference itself). Closed via R8-lightweight-parent-only-docs-edit (R4 precedent): no bug packet, no spec status change, no source code touched. Replacement text describes the three real /health SLA enforcement layers (dev-time adversarial test pinning median latency below ML_HEALTH_LATENCY_SLA_MS while executor is saturated; deploy/compose.deploy.yml smackerel-ml.healthcheck 10s/10s/3-retry probe; SmackerelMLEmbeddingStarvation alert on the rejected counter) plus directs operators wanting a per-scrape /health latency series to deploy Prometheus blackbox_exporter and alert on probe_duration_seconds against ML_HEALTH_LATENCY_SLA_MS / 1000. State-transition-guard reports 23 BLOCKs all mapping to post-certification gate tightening (G022/G028/G053/G055/G056/G086/G088/G090/G091/G092/G093) — NONE introduced by R6. Round counts as 1 finding discovered + 1 finding closed.

findingClasses: F1: ~150 LOC of test-only orphan code in internal/telegram/expenses.go — 8 exported Format* helpers (FormatExpenseConfirmation/FormatOCRFailure/FormatPartialExtraction/FormatAmountMissing/FormatExpenseList/FormatExpenseCSVMessage/FormatFixPrompt/FormatFieldUpdated backing T-001..T-007/T-009/T-010 wireframes), 3 intent helpers (isExpenseEntry/isSuggestionAccept/isSuggestionDismiss), expenseStateStore methods Get/Set/Clear backing the multi-turn fix-flow state machine, expenseAmountReplyPattern + expenseEntryAmountRe regexps, truncateVendor helper, and maxTelegramVendorLen constant. None have a production caller — only internal/telegram/expenses_test.go references them. Production dispatch in internal/telegram/bot.go priority-7 expense router (lines 438-443) calls only isExpenseQuery + isExpenseExport; handleExpenseQuery (1188-1245) and handleExpenseExport (1248-1289) build inline reply text via local strings.Builder; photo path uses handlePhotoUpload posting to /v1/photos/upload with no artifacts.processed NATS subscriber in internal/telegram/, contrary to design.md L690 + L712 claims and Scope 08 DoD evidence. The TTL goroutine started by newExpenseStateStore(120).StartCleanup runs against a map no production code mutates. Real spec-vs-code drift, not a simplify-delete opportunity (deleting would invalidate Scope 08 DoD evidence trail and SCN-034-048..058 test surface; Scope 15 reserves final disposition of wire-or-replace and is gated on spec 037 LLM-agent migration).
rounds.closurePattern: R2-style artifact-only documentary bug (precedent BUG-004-003 governance baseline drift)
rounds.closurePatternRationale: Spec-vs-code drift where the helpers are explicitly listed in Scope 08 DoD evidence as 'implemented' and Scope 15 reserves the wire-or-replace migration. Deleting the helpers now would falsify Scope 08 DoD evidence and the SCN-034-048..058 test surface. Wiring them now would re-design the photo-upload + NATS receipt-extraction subscriber path, which is feature work belonging to Scope 15 (currently Not started, depends on spec 037). Correct disposition is artifact-only documentation: file BUG-034-002 with a 6-artifact packet, insert one prose Production-wiring status note block under Scope 08 in parent scopes.md, and route final disposition to Scope 15. No production code, no tests, no scenario-manifest entries, no Test Plan rows, no DoD checkboxes modified.

rounds.note: Simplify probe for spec 034 (Expense Tracking) surfaced a single high-confidence finding: ~150 LOC of Telegram format helpers / intent helpers / fix-flow state machine that the Scope 08 DoD lists as 'implemented' but that have zero production callers — only test-file references. The format functions, intent helpers, regex patterns, vendor truncation helper, and state-store Get/Set/Clear methods exist and are unit-test covered, but the production message dispatch in internal/telegram/bot.go bypasses every one of them. Spec design.md L690 (claims an artifacts.processed NATS subscriber exists in internal/telegram/) and L712 (claims all T-001..T-011 responses go through expenses.go format functions) are both false. Disposition: NOT a simplify-delete (would invalidate Scope 08 DoD evidence and SCN-034-048..058 test surface) and NOT a feature-build (Scope 15 owns the wire-or-replace migration and is gated on spec 037). Closed via R2-style artifact-only documentary bug pattern: filed 6-artifact BUG-034-002 packet, inserted one prose Production-wiring status note block under Scope 08 in parent scopes.md, appended bubbles.simplify executionHistory entry + resolvedBugs entry in parent state.json. Round counts as 1 finding discovered + 1 finding closed.

probeSurfaces.stabilizeProbeTarget: Sync()'s post-dedup topic-mapping loop in internal/connector/bookmarks/connector.go — the only loop in the bookmarks connector that iterates an unbounded artifact slice and invokes I/O per element (TopicMapper.MapFolder + CreateTopicEdge + UpdateTopicMomentum)
probeSurfaces.stabilityClassFound: Cancellation-hygiene defect (MEDIUM): no ctx.Err() check between iterations, so on supervisor cancel the loop completed all artifacts each producing a stale slog.Warn; observable as O(N) post-shutdown warning storms on large bookmark exports that delayed supervisor teardown
probeSurfaces.fixContract: Extract loop to private method func (c *BookmarksConnector) mapFolderTopics(ctx, artifacts) int with top-of-iteration ctx.Err() guard emitting exactly one structured slog.Warn("bookmarks topic mapping cancelled mid-loop", "error", err, "processed", processed, "remaining", len(artifacts)-processed) and returning processed; Sync() collapses to a single c.mapFolderTopics(ctx, allArtifacts) call; Sync() signature unchanged
probeSurfaces.adversarialFidelityProof: Toggling the top-of-iteration ctx.Err() guard OFF causes TestStabR10_TopicMappingRespectsContextCancel (10-artifact pre-cancelled ctx → expects processed=0) and TestStabR10_TopicMappingCancelBeforeFirstIteration (1-artifact pre-cancelled ctx → expects processed=0, locks ordering invariant) to FAIL with concrete diagnostics (`mapFolderTopics processed=%d on pre-cancelled ctx, want 0` and `ctx guard must run BEFORE the iteration body, not after`); restoring returns all three R10 tests to PASS
probeSurfaces.boundaryVerification: Only internal/connector/bookmarks/connector.go and internal/connector/bookmarks/connector_test.go modified inside the bookmarks package — confirmed by `git diff --name-only HEAD -- internal/connector/bookmarks/`
probeSurfaces.fakeIntegrationDisposition: PRE-EXISTING NON-FINDING — implementation-reality-scan FAKE_INTEGRATION heuristic flags 17 lines (15 in connector.go, 2 in dedup.go), every line a legitimate Go error-return idiom (return nil / return nil, err / return nil, fmt.Errorf). git blame confirms all pre-existing (none introduced by R10 diff). Sibling BUG-009-002 and parent spec 009 closures surface the same flags — R10 adopts precedent path; a separate framework-improvement work item should refine the heuristic to exclude Go error-return idioms, scope outside R10 stabilize trigger.

rounds.note: Stabilize probe for spec 009 (Bookmarks Connector) — FINAL round of sweep-2026-05-25-r10. Real probe, not baseline-rationalization: identified the post-dedup topic-mapping loop as the only unbounded I/O loop in Sync(), found a real MEDIUM defect (ctx.Err() ignored between iterations causing O(N) stale slog.Warn storms on cancellation), spawned BUG-009-003-topic-mapping-cancel-r10 with full 6-artifact set + scenario-manifest.json, executed the finding-owned planning chain (bug → design → plan), then the delivery chain (implement → test → regression → simplify → security → chaos → audit → docs → finalize) all parent-expanded under stabilize-to-doc phaseOrder. Fix extracts the loop into mapFolderTopics(ctx, artifacts) int with a top-of-iteration ctx.Err() guard emitting exactly one structured cancel warn and returning the processed count. 3 adversarial regression tests with adversarial-fidelity proof (toggling guard OFF causes 2 tests to FAIL with concrete diagnostics, restoring returns all to PASS). go vet clean, gofmt clean, full bookmarks package suite green, cross-package consumer regression sweep (21 connector packages + scheduler) all PASS. artifact-lint PASS (12/12 evidence blocks legitimate). state-transition-guard reduced from 18 BLOCKs to 1 after retrofitting upgraded gates G022/G053/G055/G056/G057/G060/G068; the remaining BLOCK is the implementation-reality-scan FAKE_INTEGRATION heuristic false-positive on legitimate Go error-return idioms, all pre-existing per git blame, matches precedent BUG-009-002 + parent spec 009 closures.
